rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL RHODES VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 26, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Golden Gate Drywall's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's causes of action for negligence and strict liability is off calendar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant did not demonstrate that plaintiff failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive written discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his claim. Defendant did not demonstrate that it sufficiently questioned plaintiff during his deposition regarding the work he saw defendant's employees perform at any time prior to the Hewlett Packard site in 1977. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden by negating an essential element of plaintiff's claim. Even if defendant successfully demonstrated that all Kaiser Gypsum drywall accessory products did not contain asbestos as of 1975, defendant did not demonstrate that its employees never exposed plaintiff to Kaiser Gypsum drywall accessory products prior to 1975. Plaintiff has dismissed his negligence and strict liability causes of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CHERYL UNTERMANN VS. ADBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 26, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant W.W. Grainger, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party's request. = (503/TLJ)
